$comment: Project-scoped Claude Code settings. Lives at .claude/settings.json so the whole team
shares the same hooks, MCP servers, and permissions when they clone the repo.

permissions.$comment: Allow common dev commands without prompting; require approval for anything that touches credentials, the network broadly, or the warehouse outside the project path.

hooks.description: Block destructive SQL (DROP/TRUNCATE/unqualified DELETE/ALTER DROP COLUMN) unless the operator includes I_HAVE_BACKED_UP. Exit 2 hard-stops the tool call.
Scan staging-model SQL writes for raw PII columns. Blocks the write if email/phone/ssn/dob/address appear unhashed.
Run sqlfluff (for .sql) or ruff (for .py) after every write. Non-blocking — keeps the codebase formatted without nagging Claude during a multi-step edit.

$comment: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers give Claude structured access to external systems.
- 'warehouse': read-only DuckDB connection so Claude can describe tables, run safe SELECTs,
and check row counts without us having to expose a generic Bash shell.
- 'filesystem': scoped to the data/ directory so Claude can browse raw partitions safely.
Both run locally. No credentials leave the developer's machine.

warehouse.description: Read-only DuckDB MCP server. Exposes list_tables, describe_table, and read_query tools. The readonly flag is enforced at the connection level — a layered defense alongside the pre-sql-execute hook.

filesystem.description: Filesystem MCP server scoped to ./data. Lets Claude inspect raw partitions and processed outputs without granting unrestricted file access.
